rawText:
In any debate worth having, there are usually at least two reasonable positions, and the ability to summarize both sides fairly is a skill that earns trust.

When people feel that their view has been understood — even if the other person disagrees — they are far more willing to listen in return.

A fair summary starts by stating each side in the strongest terms its supporters would use, rather than in the weakest terms its critics would choose.

This principle is sometimes called the "steel man" approach: instead of attacking a straw man version of the argument, you build the best version before responding.

On the one hand, supporters of remote work argue that employees are more productive when they can control their environment and eliminate long commutes.

On the other hand, those who favor office work point out that spontaneous conversations and shared physical space build the kind of trust that video calls struggle to replicate.

Notice that neither summary uses loaded words like "lazy" or "controlling" — each side is presented in terms that its own supporters would recognize and accept.

The transition between the two sides matters just as much as the content itself.

Phrases like "on the one hand … on the other hand," "while some argue … others believe," and "supporters say … however, critics respond" signal to the reader that you are being balanced.

Fairness does not mean that both sides are equally correct — it means that both sides are honestly represented before you offer your own judgment.

After presenting both views, you can state which one you find more convincing and explain why, without pretending that the other side has no merit.

A sentence like "While I find the productivity evidence for remote work compelling, I also recognize that team culture is harder to maintain without in-person contact" shows that your opinion is informed, not one-sided.

People who can summarize opposing views fairly tend to be more persuasive, because audiences trust speakers who demonstrate that they have considered the full picture.

The goal is not to avoid having an opinion but to show that your opinion was reached through honest thinking rather than selective listening.

sentences.text: When people argue, there are usually two sides to think about.
sentences.translation: 사람들이 논쟁할 때, 보통 생각해 볼 두 가지 입장이 있어요.

sentences.text: It is important to explain both sides nicely, not just your own.
sentences.translation: 내 입장만이 아니라, 양쪽 모두를 공정하게 설명하는 것이 중요해요.

sentences.text: Some people like working from home, and some like going to an office.
sentences.translation: 어떤 사람들은 재택근무를 좋아하고, 어떤 사람들은 사무실 출근을 좋아해요.

sentences.text: You should not use mean words when talking about the other side.
sentences.translation: 상대편 이야기를 할 때 나쁜 말을 쓰면 안 돼요.

sentences.text: After explaining both sides, you can say which one you agree with.
sentences.translation: 양쪽을 다 설명한 뒤에, 어느 쪽에 동의하는지 말할 수 있어요.

sentences.text: People will trust you more if you show that you listened to everyone.
sentences.translation: 모두의 말을 들었다는 것을 보여주면, 사람들이 여러분을 더 믿어줄 거예요.

sentences.text: The article explains why summarizing both sides of a debate fairly is a valuable communication skill.
sentences.translation: 이 글은 토론의 양측을 공정하게 요약하는 것이 왜 소중한 소통 기술인지 설명해요.

sentences.text: A "steel man" approach means presenting the other side's argument in its strongest form, not its weakest.
sentences.translation: "스틸맨" 접근법은 상대방의 주장을 가장 약한 형태가 아니라 가장 강한 형태로 제시하는 거예요.

sentences.text: The writer uses remote work versus office work as an example of balanced summary.
sentences.translation: 글쓴이는 재택근무 대 사무실 근무를 균형 잡힌 요약의 예시로 사용해요.

sentences.text: Transition phrases like "on the one hand … on the other hand" help signal fairness to the reader.
sentences.translation: "한편으로는 … 다른 한편으로는" 같은 전환 구문이 독자에게 공정함을 알려 줘요.

sentences.text: Fairness does not require agreeing with both sides equally — it requires representing them honestly.
sentences.translation: 공정함은 양쪽에 똑같이 동의하는 게 아니라, 양쪽을 솔직하게 대변하는 거예요.

sentences.text: People who show they have considered opposing views tend to be more persuasive and trusted.
sentences.translation: 반대 의견을 고려했음을 보여주는 사람이 더 설득력 있고 신뢰받는 경향이 있어요.

sentences.text: This essay models a meta-rhetorical approach: it teaches balanced argumentation while simultaneously demonstrating the technique.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이는 메타 수사적 접근을 보여줘요: 균형 잡힌 논증을 가르치면서 동시에 그 기법을 직접 시연하고 있어요.

sentences.text: The "steel man" concept — the opposite of a straw man — is introduced as the ethical foundation of fair summary writing.
sentences.translation: "스틸맨" 개념 — 허수아비 논증의 반대 — 이 공정한 요약 글쓰기의 윤리적 토대로 제시돼요.

sentences.text: The remote-work versus office-work example functions as an embedded demonstration, giving the reader a concrete model of the abstract principle.
sentences.translation: 재택근무 대 사무실 근무 사례는 내재적 시연으로 기능하며, 독자에게 추상적 원리의 구체적 모델을 제공해요.

sentences.text: Discourse markers such as "on the one hand / on the other hand" and "while … others" are catalogued explicitly, making the essay both instructional and analytical.
sentences.translation: "한편 / 다른 한편"이나 "~인 반면 … 다른 이들은" 같은 담화 표지가 명시적으로 목록화되어, 에세이가 교육적이면서도 분석적이에요.

sentences.text: The distinction between fairness-as-equal-validity and fairness-as-honest-representation is a nuanced rhetorical move that prevents false equivalence.
sentences.translation: 동등한 타당성으로서의 공정과 솔직한 대변으로서의 공정의 구분은, 잘못된 등치를 방지하는 섬세한 수사적 장치예요.

sentences.text: The closing argument — that the goal is honest thinking, not neutrality — reframes persuasion as an intellectual virtue rather than a manipulation tactic.
sentences.translation: 마무리 주장 — 목표는 중립이 아니라 정직한 사고라는 것 — 은 설득을 조작 기술이 아닌 지적 덕목으로 재규정해요.

sentences.original: In any debate worth having, there are usually at least two reasonable positions, and the ability to summarize both sides fairly is a skill that earns trust.
sentences.translation: 할 가치가 있는 어떤 토론에서든, 보통 최소 두 개의 합리적 입장이 존재하며, 양쪽을 공정하게 요약하는 능력은 신뢰를 얻는 기술이다.

grammarElements.text: usually at least two reasonable positions
grammarElements.label: 진주어

grammarElements.text: the ability to summarize both sides fairly
grammarElements.label: 주어 (to 부정사 수식)

grammarElements.text: is a skill that earns trust
grammarElements.label: 보어 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: The debate lasted over an hour.
Both sides had reasonable arguments.
Can you summarize the main points?

expressions.usage: "worth + 동명사"로 ~할 만한 가치가 있다는 의미를 나타낸다
행동이나 능력으로 인해 타인의 신뢰를 확보할 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: In any [noun] worth [gerund], there are [number] [adjective] [noun], and [ability] is a skill that [verb] [noun]
patterns.explanation: 가치 있는 맥락을 설정하고, 그 안에 존재하는 요소를 제시한 뒤, 핵심 능력을 기술로 규정하는 도입 패턴.
examples: In any job worth keeping, there are always new skills to learn, and the ability to adapt quickly is a skill that drives success.
In any friendship worth maintaining, there are moments of disagreement, and the ability to listen patiently is a skill that deepens bonds.

drillQuestions.question: In any debate worth _____, there are at least two sides.
The ability to summarize both sides fairly is a skill that _____ trust.
"Reasonable positions" means positions that are _____.
"Worth having" uses the pattern "worth + _____."

sentences.notes: 글 전체의 주제를 도입하는 첫 문장. "worth having"으로 가치 있는 토론이라는 전제를 세우고, "earns trust"로 공정한 요약의 실용적 보상을 제시한다. there are 구문과 관계절이 결합된 복합 구조이다.

koreanAlignment.en: In any debate worth having
there are usually at least two reasonable positions
koreanAlignment.ko: 보통 최소 두 개의 합리적 입장이 존재하며

koreanAlignment.en: the ability to summarize both sides fairly
is a skill that earns trust

pronunciationNotes: debate: /dɪˈbeɪt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "디베잇"에 가깝다.
summarize: /ˈsʌməraɪz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "써머라이즈"에 가깝다. -ize를 /aɪz/로 발음한다.

sentences.original: When people feel that their view has been understood — even if the other person disagrees — they are far more willing to listen in return.
sentences.translation: 사람들은 자신의 견해가 이해받았다고 느낄 때 — 상대가 동의하지 않더라도 — 훨씬 더 기꺼이 상대의 말을 경청한다.

grammarElements.text: When people feel that their view has been understood
grammarElements.label: 시간절 (When + that절 + 수동태)

grammarElements.text: even if the other person disagrees
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (even if)

grammarElements.text: are far more willing to listen
grammarElements.label: 동사 (be willing to + 비교급 강조)

vocabulary.example: She wanted to feel understood by her team.
He disagrees with the proposal.
She is willing to compromise.

expressions.usage: 양보의 의미로, 조건이 충족되어도 결과가 변하지 않음을 나타낸다
상대의 행동에 대한 반응이나 보답을 나타낼 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: When people feel that [subject] has been [past participle] — even if [concession] — they are far more willing to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 사람들의 심리적 조건을 제시하고, 양보절로 반대 상황을 인정한 뒤에도 결과가 유지됨을 보여주는 패턴.
examples: When people feel that their effort has been recognized — even if the result is not perfect — they are far more willing to try again.
When people feel that their complaint has been heard — even if the solution takes time — they are far more willing to be patient.

drillQuestions.question: When people feel that their view has been _____, they listen more.
"Even if the other person disagrees" is a _____ clause.
They are _____ more willing to listen in return.
"In return" means _____.

sentences.notes: 대시(—)로 삽입된 양보절(even if ~ disagrees)이 핵심 주장을 더 강하게 만든다. "far more willing"에서 far가 비교급을 강조하여 경청의 효과를 부각한다.

koreanAlignment.en: When people feel that their view has been understood
koreanAlignment.ko: 사람들은 자신의 견해가 이해받았다고 느낄 때

koreanAlignment.en: even if the other person disagrees
they are far more willing to listen
in return

pronunciationNotes: understood: /ˌʌndərˈstʊd/ — 세 번째 음절에 강세. "언더스투드"에 가깝다.
willing: /ˈwɪlɪŋ/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "윌링"에 가깝다. -ing을 "잉"이 아닌 /ɪŋ/으로 발음한다.

sentences.original: A fair summary starts by stating each side in the strongest terms its supporters would use, rather than in the weakest terms its critics would choose.
sentences.translation: 공정한 요약은 비판자들이 선택할 가장 약한 표현이 아니라, 지지자들이 사용할 가장 강한 표현으로 각 입장을 서술하는 것에서 시작한다.

grammarElements.text: stating each side in the strongest terms
grammarElements.label: 동명사구 (수단)

grammarElements.text: rather than in the weakest terms its critics would choose
grammarElements.label: rather than 대조구

vocabulary.example: Please write a brief summary of the article.
She began by stating her main argument.
Supporters of the plan gathered outside.

expressions.usage: 첫 번째 단계나 방법을 제시할 때 쓰는 표현
두 가지를 대비시켜 선호하는 쪽을 나타낼 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: [Subject] starts by [gerund] in the [superlative] terms [supporters/critics] would [verb], rather than in the [superlative] terms [the other group] would [verb]
patterns.explanation: 대비되는 두 기준(최강 표현 vs. 최약 표현)을 rather than으로 나란히 놓아 올바른 방법을 제시하는 패턴.
examples: A good journalist starts by quoting sources in the most accurate terms they would accept, rather than in the most sensational terms critics would prefer.
A strong essay starts by framing the opposition in the most charitable terms their advocates would use, rather than in the most dismissive terms opponents would pick.

drillQuestions.question: A fair summary starts _____ stating each side in the strongest terms.
You should use the strongest terms its _____ would use.
"Rather than" introduces a _____.
"The weakest terms" is the opposite of "the _____ terms."

sentences.notes: "the strongest terms vs. the weakest terms" 대비로 공정한 요약의 핵심 원칙을 명확히 한다. rather than이 잘못된 방식을 병치시켜 올바른 방법을 부각하는 설명 전략이다.

koreanAlignment.en: A fair summary starts by
stating each side in the strongest terms
koreanAlignment.ko: 가장 강한 표현으로 각 입장을 서술하는 것

koreanAlignment.en: its supporters would use
rather than in the weakest terms its critics would choose
koreanAlignment.ko: 비판자들이 선택할 가장 약한 표현이 아니라

pronunciationNotes: summary: /ˈsʌməri/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "써머리"에 가깝다. summer와 발음이 비슷하다.
strongest: /ˈstrɔːŋɡɪst/ — str을 한 번에 발음. "스트롱기스트"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: This principle is sometimes called the "steel man" approach: instead of attacking a straw man version of the argument, you build the best version before responding.
sentences.translation: 이 원칙은 때때로 "스틸맨" 접근법이라 불린다: 논점의 허수아비 버전을 공격하는 대신, 반응하기 전에 가장 좋은 버전을 구축하는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: instead of attacking a straw man version of the argument
grammarElements.label: instead of + 동명사구

grammarElements.text: you build the best version before responding
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 설명절

vocabulary.example: This is a basic principle of fair debate.
A steel man argument is the opposite of a straw man.
Do not attack a straw man version of the argument.

expressions.usage: 어떤 행위를 하지 않고 다른 행위를 선택할 때 쓰는 전치사 표현
순서를 나타내며 먼저 해야 할 일을 강조하는 표현

patterns.pattern: This [noun] is sometimes called the "[name]" approach: instead of [gerund A], you [verb B] before [gerund C]
patterns.explanation: 개념에 이름을 붙인 뒤 콜론으로 설명을 이어가며, instead of와 before로 행동 순서를 대비하는 패턴.
examples: This strategy is sometimes called the "listen first" approach: instead of jumping to solutions, you understand the problem before suggesting fixes.
This method is sometimes called the "empathy" approach: instead of judging behavior, you explore the reasons before reacting.

drillQuestions.question: This principle is sometimes _____ the "steel man" approach.
_____ of attacking a straw man, you build the best version.
A "straw man" is a _____ version of someone's argument.
You build the best version _____ responding.

sentences.notes: 콜론(:) 뒤에 "steel man"과 "straw man"의 대비를 통해 핵심 개념을 설명한다. "instead of ~ you ~" 구조가 잘못된 방법과 올바른 방법을 명확히 구분한다.

koreanAlignment.en: This principle is sometimes called
the "steel man" approach
instead of attacking a straw man version of the argument
you build the best version before responding
koreanAlignment.ko: 반응하기 전에 가장 좋은 버전을 구축하는 것이다

pronunciationNotes: principle: /ˈprɪnsəpl/ — 첫 음절에 강세. principal과 발음이 같다. "프린서플"에 가깝다.
approach: /əˈproʊtʃ/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어프로우치"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: On the one hand, supporters of remote work argue that employees are more productive when they can control their environment and eliminate long commutes.
sentences.translation: 한편으로, 재택근무 지지자들은 직원들이 자신의 환경을 통제하고 긴 통근을 없앨 수 있을 때 더 생산적이라고 주장한다.

grammarElements.text: when they can control their environment and eliminate long commutes
grammarElements.label: 시간/조건절 (when)

vocabulary.example: Remote work became common during the pandemic.
She feels more productive at home.
The new route eliminates unnecessary stops.
Long commutes can cause stress.

expressions.usage: 두 가지 대조되는 입장 중 첫 번째를 소개할 때 쓰는 담화 표지
업무 조건을 자율적으로 조절할 수 있다는 뜻으로 쓴다

patterns.pattern: On the one hand, supporters of [position A] argue that [claim], when [condition]
patterns.explanation: 대비 구조의 첫 부분으로, 한쪽 입장의 지지자가 무엇을 주장하는지 when절의 조건과 함께 제시하는 패턴.
examples: On the one hand, supporters of public transit argue that cities become cleaner when people share rides.
On the one hand, supporters of online learning argue that students learn faster when they can study at their own pace.

drillQuestions.question: On the one hand, supporters of remote work _____ that employees are more productive.
Employees are more productive when they can _____ their environment.
"On the one hand" is typically paired with "_____."
"Eliminate long commutes" means to _____ them.

sentences.notes: "On the one hand"로 대비 구조의 첫 번째 입장을 시작한다. 재택근무 지지자의 주장을 "more productive"와 "control / eliminate"라는 구체적 근거와 함께 공정하게 제시한다.

koreanAlignment.en: On the one hand
supporters of remote work argue
that employees are more productive
when they can control their environment and eliminate long commutes
koreanAlignment.ko: 자신의 환경을 통제하고 긴 통근을 없앨 수 있을 때

pronunciationNotes: productive: /prəˈdʌktɪv/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "프러덕티브"에 가깝다.
eliminate: /ɪˈlɪmɪneɪt/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "일리미네잇"에 가깝다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대비 구조의 첫 축 (first arm of contrast)

sentences.original: On the other hand, those who favor office work point out that spontaneous conversations and shared physical space build the kind of trust that video calls struggle to replicate.
sentences.translation: 다른 한편으로, 사무실 근무를 선호하는 사람들은 자발적인 대화와 공유된 물리적 공간이 화상 통화로는 재현하기 어려운 종류의 신뢰를 형성한다고 지적한다.

grammarElements.text: that spontaneous conversations and shared physical space build the kind of trust
grammarElements.label: that 명사절

grammarElements.text: that video calls struggle to replicate
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (trust 수식)

vocabulary.example: Those who favor the plan spoke first.
Spontaneous conversations often lead to great ideas.
It is hard to replicate in-person chemistry online.

expressions.usage: "on the one hand"에 대응하여 반대 입장을 소개할 때 쓰는 담화 표지
중요한 사실이나 관점을 강조하여 언급할 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: On the other hand, those who favor [position B] point out that [fact A] build(s) the kind of [noun] that [noun] struggle(s) to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 대비의 두 번째 축으로, 반대측 주장을 "the kind of ~ that ~ struggle to ~"라는 구체적 근거와 함께 공정하게 소개하는 패턴.
examples: On the other hand, those who favor in-person classes point out that face-to-face interaction builds the kind of rapport that online platforms struggle to create.
On the other hand, those who favor traditional retail point out that physical stores offer the kind of experience that websites struggle to match.

drillQuestions.question: On the other hand, those who _____ office work point out a different view.
Spontaneous conversations build the kind of trust that video calls _____ to replicate.
"On the other hand" signals the _____ side of a debate.
"Replicate" means to _____.

sentences.notes: "On the other hand"로 대비의 두 번째 축을 완성한다. "the kind of trust that video calls struggle to replicate"라는 이중 관계절이 사무실 근무의 장점을 설득력 있게 표현한다.

koreanAlignment.en: On the other hand
those who favor office work point out
that spontaneous conversations and shared physical space
build the kind of trust
that video calls struggle to replicate

pronunciationNotes: spontaneous: /spɑːnˈteɪniəs/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "스판테이니어스"에 가깝다.
replicate: /ˈreplɪkeɪt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "레플리케잇"에 가깝다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대비 구조의 두 번째 축 (second arm of contrast)

sentences.original: Notice that neither summary uses loaded words like "lazy" or "controlling" — each side is presented in terms that its own supporters would recognize and accept.
sentences.translation: 어느 쪽 요약도 "게으른"이나 "통제적인" 같은 편향된 단어를 사용하지 않는다는 점에 주목하라 — 각 입장은 그 지지자들이 인정하고 받아들일 표현으로 제시된다.

grammarElements.text: that neither summary uses loaded words like "lazy" or "controlling"
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 (neither + 명사)

grammarElements.text: in terms that its own supporters would recognize and accept
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계절

vocabulary.example: Avoid using loaded words in a neutral summary.
Neither option was perfect.
They would recognize this as a fair description.

expressions.usage: 독자의 주의를 환기시키며 중요한 관찰을 제시할 때 쓰는 명령형 표현
감정적으로 치우친 언어 사용을 지적할 때 쓰는 수사학 용어

patterns.pattern: Notice that neither [noun] uses [type of language] — each [noun] is presented in terms that [group] would [verb]
patterns.explanation: 독자에게 공정한 표현의 특징을 관찰하도록 유도하고, 대시 뒤에 그 원칙을 재확인하는 메타 설명 패턴.
examples: Notice that neither review uses emotional language — each product is described in terms that the manufacturer would accept.
Notice that neither report uses accusatory language — each incident is presented in terms that both parties would agree on.

drillQuestions.question: Notice that _____ summary uses loaded words.
"Loaded words" are words that carry strong _____.
Each side is presented in terms that its supporters would _____ and accept.
"Lazy" and "controlling" are examples of _____ words.

sentences.notes: "Notice that"으로 독자에게 직접 말을 건네는 메타 해설 문장. "loaded words"라는 수사학 용어를 구체적 예("lazy", "controlling")와 함께 제시하며, 대시 뒤에서 공정 요약의 기준을 재확인한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Notice that neither summary uses loaded words
koreanAlignment.ko: 어느 쪽 요약도 편향된 단어를 사용하지 않는다는 점에 주목하라

koreanAlignment.en: like "lazy" or "controlling"
each side is presented in terms
that its own supporters would recognize and accept

pronunciationNotes: neither: /ˈniːðər/ 또는 /ˈnaɪðər/ — 미국식은 /ˈniːðər/, 영국식은 /ˈnaɪðər/ 둘 다 쓴다.
recognize: /ˈrekəɡnaɪz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "레커그나이즈"에 가깝다. g는 거의 묵음.

sentences.original: The transition between the two sides matters just as much as the content itself.
sentences.translation: 두 입장 사이의 전환은 내용 자체만큼이나 중요하다.

grammarElements.text: The transition between the two sides
grammarElements.label: 주어 (전치사 수식)

grammarElements.text: just as much as the content itself
grammarElements.label: 동등 비교 (as ~ as)

vocabulary.example: The transition between paragraphs should be smooth.
Every detail matters in a good essay.
The content of the report was well researched.

expressions.usage: 두 가지가 동일한 정도로 중요함을 나타내는 동등 비교 표현
itself를 붙여 다른 요소가 아닌 바로 그것임을 강조한다

patterns.pattern: The [noun A] between [noun B] matters just as much as [noun C] itself
patterns.explanation: 흔히 간과되는 요소(A)가 핵심 요소(C)만큼 중요하다는 점을 강조하는 동등 비교 패턴.
examples: The space between paragraphs matters just as much as the writing itself.
The tone between colleagues matters just as much as the feedback itself.

drillQuestions.question: The transition matters just as _____ as the content.
"The content itself" emphasizes the content and nothing _____.
"Matters" in this sentence means _____.
The transition is between the _____ sides.

sentences.notes: 짧고 간결한 주장으로, "just as much as"의 동등 비교를 통해 전환 표현의 중요성을 내용과 동일 선상에 놓는다. 다음 문장에서 구체적 전환 표현을 나열하기 위한 연결 문장이다.

koreanAlignment.en: The transition between the two sides
matters just as much as
the content itself

pronunciationNotes: transition: /trænˈzɪʃən/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "트랜지션"에 가깝다.
matters: /ˈmætərz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. t가 미국식에서 /ɾ/ (플랩 t)로 발음되어 "매러즈"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: Phrases like "on the one hand … on the other hand," "while some argue … others believe," and "supporters say … however, critics respond" signal to the reader that you are being balanced.
sentences.translation: "한편으로 … 다른 한편으로", "일부는 주장하는 반면 … 다른 이들은 믿는다", "지지자들은 말한다 … 그러나 비판자들은 반응한다" 같은 표현들은 독자에게 여러분이 균형 잡힌 태도를 취하고 있다는 신호를 보낸다.

grammarElements.text: Phrases like "on the one hand … on the other hand," "while some argue … others believe," and "supporters say … however, critics respond"
grammarElements.label: 주어 (삼중 나열 예시)

vocabulary.example: Use transitional phrases to connect ideas.
These words signal a change in tone.
A balanced argument considers both sides.

expressions.usage: 글의 표현이 독자에게 특정 메시지를 전달한다는 뜻으로 쓰는 수사학적 표현

expressions.meaning: 일부는 주장하는 반면 … 다른 이들은 믿는다
expressions.usage: 대비되는 두 입장을 균형 있게 소개할 때 쓰는 전환 표현

patterns.pattern: Phrases like "[example A]," "[example B]," and "[example C]" signal to the reader that [message]
patterns.explanation: 세 가지 구체적 예시를 나열한 뒤, 그것들이 독자에게 보내는 메시지를 설명하는 교육적 패턴.
examples: Phrases like "however," "on the contrary," and "nevertheless" signal to the reader that a counterpoint is coming.
Phrases like "for example," "such as," and "to illustrate" signal to the reader that evidence will follow.

drillQuestions.question: These phrases _____ to the reader that you are being balanced.
"On the one hand … on the other hand" is a _____ pair.
"You are being balanced" uses the present _____ tense.
The sentence lists _____ example phrases.

sentences.notes: 세 가지 전환 표현 쌍을 삼중 나열로 제시하는 교육적 문장. "signal to the reader"로 이러한 표현들이 단순한 장식이 아니라 소통 기능을 한다는 점을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Phrases like "on the one hand … on the other hand,"
"while some argue … others believe,"
and "supporters say … however, critics respond"
signal to the reader that you are being balanced
koreanAlignment.ko: 독자에게 균형 잡힌 태도를 취하고 있다는 신호를 보낸다

pronunciationNotes: phrases: /ˈfreɪzɪz/ — ph가 /f/로 발음된다. "프레이지즈"에 가깝다.
balanced: /ˈbælənst/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "밸런스트"에 가깝다. -ed가 /t/로 발음된다.

sentences.original: Fairness does not mean that both sides are equally correct — it means that both sides are honestly represented before you offer your own judgment.
sentences.translation: 공정함은 양쪽이 똑같이 옳다는 뜻이 아니다 — 여러분이 자신의 판단을 제시하기 전에 양쪽이 솔직하게 대변된다는 뜻이다.

grammarElements.text: that both sides are equally correct
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 (부정 대상)

grammarElements.text: that both sides are honestly represented before you offer your own judgment
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 (긍정 정의)

vocabulary.example: Fairness is the foundation of good debate.
Both arguments are not equally strong.
Reserve your judgment until you hear both sides.
All views should be fairly represented.

expressions.usage: 흔한 오해를 바로잡고 정확한 정의를 제시할 때 쓰는 대비 구문
의견을 공식적으로 밝힐 때 쓰는 표현

patterns.pattern: [Abstract noun] does not mean that [misconception] — it means that [correct definition]
patterns.explanation: 추상적 개념에 대한 오해를 부정한 뒤, 대시로 전환하여 올바른 정의를 제시하는 재정의 패턴.
examples: Respect does not mean that you always agree — it means that you listen before judging.
Freedom does not mean that there are no rules — it means that rules protect everyone equally.

drillQuestions.question: Fairness does not mean that both sides are _____ correct.
It means that both sides are honestly _____.
"Does not mean … it means" is used to correct a _____.
You offer your own _____ after presenting both views.

sentences.notes: "does not mean ~ it means ~" 구조로 '공정함'의 흔한 오해(양쪽이 동등하게 옳다)를 바로잡고, 정확한 의미(솔직한 대변)를 재정의한다. 핵심 주장을 담은 문장이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Fairness does not mean
that both sides are equally correct
it means that both sides are honestly represented
before you offer your own judgment

pronunciationNotes: fairness: /ˈferənəs/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "페어니스"에 가깝다.
judgment: /ˈdʒʌdʒmənt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "저지먼트"에 가깝다. dg가 /dʒ/로 발음된다.

sentences.original: After presenting both views, you can state which one you find more convincing and explain why, without pretending that the other side has no merit.
sentences.translation: 양쪽 견해를 제시한 후, 어느 쪽이 더 설득력 있다고 생각하는지 밝히고 이유를 설명할 수 있다 — 상대편에 가치가 전혀 없는 척하지 않으면서.

grammarElements.text: which one you find more convincing
grammarElements.label: 간접의문절

grammarElements.text: without pretending that the other side has no merit
grammarElements.label: without + 동명사구

vocabulary.example: Her argument was the most convincing.
Stop pretending that you agree.
Each proposal has its own merit.

expressions.usage: 이전 단계를 완료한 다음의 순서를 나타내는 분사 표현
without + 동명사로 하지 않아야 할 행동을 조건으로 제시한다

patterns.pattern: After [gerund] both [noun], you can state which one [clause] and explain why, without [gerund] that [negative clause]
patterns.explanation: 양쪽을 검토한 뒤 입장을 밝히되, without으로 피해야 할 태도를 명시하는 절차 제시 패턴.
examples: After reviewing both proposals, you can state which one you prefer and explain why, without dismissing that the other has value.
After hearing both arguments, you can decide which one is stronger and explain why, without denying that the other has some truth.

drillQuestions.question: After presenting both views, you can _____ which one you find more convincing.
"Without pretending" means you should not _____.
"Which one you find more convincing" is an indirect _____.
The other side has no _____ means it has no value.

sentences.notes: 공정한 요약 이후의 단계를 안내하는 실용적 문장. "without pretending"이 의견 제시의 윤리적 한계를 설정한다. 간접의문절(which one you find)과 병렬 구조가 결합된 복합 문장이다.

koreanAlignment.en: After presenting both views
you can state which one you find more convincing
koreanAlignment.ko: 어느 쪽이 더 설득력 있다고 생각하는지 밝힐 수 있다

koreanAlignment.en: and explain why
without pretending that the other side has no merit
koreanAlignment.ko: 상대편에 가치가 전혀 없는 척하지 않으면서

pronunciationNotes: convincing: /kənˈvɪnsɪŋ/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컨빈싱"에 가깝다.
merit: /ˈmerɪt/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "메릿"에 가깝다. t를 살짝 발음한다.

sentences.original: A sentence like "While I find the productivity evidence for remote work compelling, I also recognize that team culture is harder to maintain without in-person contact" shows that your opinion is informed, not one-sided.
sentences.translation: "재택근무의 생산성 근거가 설득력 있다고 생각하지만, 대면 접촉 없이는 팀 문화를 유지하기 더 어렵다는 점도 인정한다"와 같은 문장은 여러분의 의견이 편향적이 아니라 정보에 기반한 것임을 보여 준다.

grammarElements.text: A sentence like "While I find … contact"
grammarElements.label: 주어 (like + 인용문 예시)

grammarElements.text: that your opinion is informed, not one-sided
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 (대비적 보어)

vocabulary.example: The evidence is quite compelling.
It is hard to maintain team spirit remotely.
Make an informed decision before choosing.
A one-sided argument is less persuasive.

expressions.phrase: While I find … I also recognize
expressions.usage: 자신의 입장을 밝히면서 동시에 반대편의 타당성을 인정하는 양보 표현
의견이 한쪽으로 치우치지 않았음을 나타낼 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: A sentence like "[embedded example]" shows that [quality A], not [quality B]
patterns.explanation: 구체적 예문을 인용한 뒤, 그것이 보여주는 특성을 "A, not B" 대비로 평가하는 모범 제시 패턴.
examples: A sentence like "I see the benefit, but I worry about the cost" shows that your thinking is nuanced, not simplistic.
A sentence like "The data is promising, though more research is needed" shows that your conclusion is cautious, not reckless.

drillQuestions.question: This kind of sentence shows that your opinion is _____, not one-sided.
"While I find … I also recognize" is a _____ structure.
"Compelling" means _____ and persuasive.
"Harder to maintain without in-person contact" means in-person contact _____ team culture.

sentences.notes: 내장 예문("While I find ~ I also recognize ~")을 직접 보여주며 독자에게 모범적 표현을 시연한다. "informed, not one-sided"라는 대비가 이 기법의 효과를 간결하게 요약한다.

koreanAlignment.en: A sentence like
"While I find the productivity evidence for remote work compelling
I also recognize that team culture is harder to maintain without in-person contact"
koreanAlignment.ko: 대면 접촉 없이는 팀 문화를 유지하기 더 어렵다는 점도 인정한다"

koreanAlignment.en: shows that your opinion is informed, not one-sided
koreanAlignment.ko: 의견이 편향적이 아니라 정보에 기반한 것임을 보여 준다

pronunciationNotes: compelling: /kəmˈpelɪŋ/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "컴펠링"에 가깝다.
informed: /ɪnˈfɔːrmd/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "인폼드"에 가깝다. -ed가 /d/로 발음된다.

sentences.original: People who can summarize opposing views fairly tend to be more persuasive, because audiences trust speakers who demonstrate that they have considered the full picture.
sentences.translation: 반대 의견을 공정하게 요약할 수 있는 사람들은 더 설득력이 있는 경향이 있다, 왜냐하면 청중은 전체 그림을 고려했음을 보여주는 화자를 신뢰하기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: People who can summarize opposing views fairly
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: who demonstrate that they have considered the full picture
grammarElements.label: 관계절 + that절

vocabulary.example: She listened to opposing arguments with respect.
He is one of the most persuasive speakers I know.
She demonstrated her understanding of both sides.

expressions.usage: 일반적인 경향이나 패턴을 나타낼 때 쓴다
모든 측면을 종합적으로 파악한 상태를 나타내는 비유 표현

patterns.pattern: People who can [verb] [opposing noun] fairly tend to be more [adjective], because [audience] trust(s) [people] who demonstrate that [clause]
patterns.explanation: 공정한 태도의 실질적 보상을 제시하며, because절로 청중의 심리를 근거로 드는 설득 패턴.
examples: People who can acknowledge their mistakes fairly tend to be more respected, because colleagues trust leaders who demonstrate that they value honesty.
People who can discuss sensitive topics fairly tend to be more influential, because readers trust writers who demonstrate that they have examined multiple perspectives.

drillQuestions.question: People who summarize opposing views fairly tend to be more _____.
Audiences trust speakers who _____ that they have considered the full picture.
"Tend to be" expresses a general _____.
"The full picture" means _____.

sentences.notes: 공정한 요약의 실용적 이점(더 높은 설득력)을 제시하는 핵심 주장. "People who ~ tend to be ~"로 일반적 경향을 밝히고, "because audiences trust speakers who ~"로 심리적 근거를 제공한다.

koreanAlignment.en: People who can summarize opposing views fairly
koreanAlignment.ko: 반대 의견을 공정하게 요약할 수 있는 사람들은

koreanAlignment.en: tend to be more persuasive
because audiences trust speakers
who demonstrate that they have considered the full picture

pronunciationNotes: persuasive: /pərˈsweɪsɪv/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "퍼스웨이시브"에 가깝다.
audiences: /ˈɔːdiənsɪz/ — 첫 음절에 강세. "오디언시즈"에 가깝다.

sentences.original: The goal is not to avoid having an opinion but to show that your opinion was reached through honest thinking rather than selective listening.
sentences.translation: 목표는 의견을 갖는 것을 피하는 게 아니라, 여러분의 의견이 선택적 경청이 아닌 정직한 사고를 통해 도달되었음을 보여주는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: not to avoid having an opinion
grammarElements.label: 보어 (부정 to 부정사 + 동명사)

grammarElements.text: that your opinion was reached through honest thinking rather than selective listening
grammarElements.label: that 명사절 (수동태 + rather than)

vocabulary.example: Do not avoid difficult conversations.
The conclusion was reached after careful analysis.
Selective listening weakens your argument.

expressions.usage: 목적의 오해를 방지하고 진정한 목적을 밝히는 대조 구문
두 가지를 대비시켜 하나를 부정하고 하나를 긍정할 때 쓴다

patterns.pattern: The goal is not to [avoid/do A] but to show that [your noun] was reached through [positive process] rather than [negative process]
patterns.explanation: 글의 진정한 목적을 밝히며, "not to ~ but to ~"로 오해를 차단하고 "rather than"으로 올바른 과정과 잘못된 과정을 대비하는 결론 패턴.
examples: The goal is not to eliminate disagreement but to show that your position was reached through careful research rather than blind loyalty.
The goal is not to silence critics but to show that your decision was reached through open discussion rather than closed-door politics.

drillQuestions.question: The goal is not to _____ having an opinion.
Your opinion was reached through honest thinking _____ than selective listening.
"Not to … but to" clarifies the true _____ of the essay.
"Selective listening" means listening only to what _____ your view.

sentences.notes: 글 전체의 결론이자 핵심 메시지. "not to ~ but to ~" 구조가 첫 문장의 주제를 마무리하며, "honest thinking rather than selective listening"이라는 대비가 전체 논지를 응축한다. 수동태(was reached)로 의견 형성 과정의 객관성을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The goal is not to avoid having an opinion
koreanAlignment.ko: 목표는 의견을 갖는 것을 피하는 게 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: but to show that your opinion was reached
through honest thinking
rather than selective listening

pronunciationNotes: opinion: /əˈpɪnjən/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "어피니언"에 가깝다.
selective: /sɪˈlektɪv/ — 두 번째 음절에 강세. "실렉티브"에 가깝다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 행동 촉구 겸 재정의 (call to action with redefinition)
